calendar.summary: Buckeye Auto Logistics — Q3 Terms Negotiation
URGENT: Supply-Chain Sync — Regional Carrier Delay

messages.text: Buckeye inv attached — process Net-30, thx. Cleared on my end.
On it. Will close by EOD — no bottleneck on my side.
Tom — PO #44192 doesn't match your invoice qty. Need a corrected one before I can release. Appreciate the quick turn.
Hold payment on 44192 until I confirm the corrected invoice. Don't release yet.
Revised Buckeye inv looks right — approved, release Net-30. Thanks for waiting.
Buckeye sorted. No shipment hold. We're good.
Three routine ones attached — all approved, Net-30 across the board.
Good turnaround this week. Appreciate you keeping pace.
PO approved — forwarding for processing. Net-30.
Got your note. Open to a quiet conversation — evenings best, my personal line only. Keep it off anything work-facing.
Past-due notice came in — forwarding, process Net-30 so we don't get an account hold.
Send the JD here, I'll review quietly tonight. No calls to the office.
Forwarding now so it's queued — process Monday AM, Net-30. Don't want it slipping over the weekend.
Fwd from overnight queue — Buckeye Net-30, approved. Push it through, clearing the backlog before standup.
Mark — VP Supply Chain role, Cincinnati base, comp band well north of where you are. JD attached. Worth a confidential 20 min?
Read it. Tomorrow 7pm works — this number only, not email. Keep it quiet for now.
Another one approved, fwd to you. Don't have time to chase POs today — just get them paid so we're not the holdup.
That Buckeye invoice from day 6 — I can't match it to any PO in the system. Routing/remit info looks off too. Hold okay?
Pull the PO and verify direct with the vendor before anything moves. Should've flagged at intake. Do it today.
Quick one — confirming invoice #BK-4471, ~$48k, Net-30. Is this yours? Remit details don't reconcile on our end.
Mark, that's not us — we never sent that invoice and that account isn't ours. You'll want to lock it down.
HALT BK-4471 immediately. Vendor confirms it's fraudulent. Do not release a cent. Confirm it's stopped.
Heads up: caught a spoofed Buckeye invoice mid-process — our controls flagged the PO mismatch and payment is frozen. No loss. Writing up how it slipped intake.
Update: zero dollars out the door, account isolated, AP retraining on dual-verify for any new remit. Fully contained on my end.
Crazy week here. Still want to move on the Cincinnati conversation — what's the next step? Same line, after 6.
Long one. Can we just do the Italian place at 7:30? Don't feel like cooking the house feels too quiet anyway.
Bulk fwd of the weekend vendor batch — but verify every PO and remit before paying after that Buckeye mess. No exceptions now.
AP — these 3 are cleared, process today. Net-30 clock is ticking. Don't sit on them.
Payment halt confirmed on the Buckeye account. Resolved before EOD. No bottleneck on my end.
Thurs AM works. Use this number, not my work line. Keep it quiet for now.
Draft write-up attached re: the spoofed invoice batch. Still gut-checking the dollar figure. Don't circulate yet.
Two more URGENT Net-30s coming your way — push them through. Can't have another account hold.
Confirmed — you'll meet R. Castellano. Video link sent to your personal email. Looking strong.
Out for an appointment till 11. Hold the calendar, route anything urgent to my cell.
Backlog batch forwarded — clear them all today, I want this desk empty by Friday.
Appreciated the conversation today. The operational scope you described is exactly the challenge I'm looking for. — sent from personal
They want a final round. How's next week? They're moving fast on you.
Closing the loop — cyber-insurance claim filed on the invoice incident. Controls tightened. Handled.
Final round logistics — can we do it off-site or virtual? Can't risk it showing on my work calendar.
Pre-loading these for Monday AM — process first thing. Don't want them aging over the weekend.
Weekend batch attached. All approved, process today. Don't sit on these — vendors will start calling.
Write-up's 90% there. Two loose ends on the dock-timing column, I'll close them by EOD. It won't bottleneck the review.
Confirmed for Thu final round. Same arrangement — my cell only, nothing to the work address. I can do 11–1.
Buckeye invoice — approved, push it through. Slammed today, just clear it.
Running late, got an appointment midday. Don't hold lunch. Talk tonight.
Felt strong in there. Comp conversation is the next step — let me know what they come back with before anything's in writing.
Hey — the Buckeye one you forwarded looks like a dupe of #4471 from last week. Same amount. Want me to hold it?
Got your terms note. Half out the door right now — let's circle back Mon, I'll have a real answer.
Verbal offer is in — they're excited about you. Congrats, you earned it. Call when you can to walk through the numbers.
We need to talk this weekend. Nothing wrong, just something I've been sitting on. I'll explain at home.
I know it's a lot to spring on you. It'd mean leaving Columbus — the house, your sister, all of it. I'm not saying yes, I just can't keep doing this here. I want to figure it out together, not alone.
No pressure — they know it's a big move. Reviewing on their end, formal decision Mon. Take the weekend.
Can we talk before 9? Need to confirm numbers before I'm chained to back-to-backs all day. Use this cell, not email.
Locked in 8:30. Comp looks strong. One thing — they'll want a sense on relocation. Think about a number.
Big news tonight. Nothing bad — good kind. Let's talk after dinner, just us.
I'm in. Accepting. Send the formal paperwork — verbal yes from me tonight. Keep it to this number for now.
Pull both of those, my error. I'll verify the vendor directly before anything goes out. Don't process.
Adjuster finally called back — Thursday call scheduled. Forwarding you the details so we're on the same page.
Told them yes. Now we figure out the how and when together. I'm ready to be home more.
